name: Clara Ward
abstract: Clara Mae Ward (April 21, 1924 – January 16, 1973) was an American gospel singer who achieved great artistic and commercial success during the 1940s and 1950s, as leader of The Famous Ward Singers. A gifted singer and arranger, Ward adopted the lead-switching style, previously used primarily by male gospel quartets, creating opportunitie…

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Mahalia Jackson sang there, all of the great — God, Clara Ward, all of the great Gospel singers, and my grandmother sang in the choir and sang with these people
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I would listen to Mahalia Jackson or I would listen to The Five Satins or I’d listen to The Penguins, or whatever the records were. It was — Clara Ward.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: It was a gospel thing with Hazel Scott and Clara Ward, Nipsy Russell...
